mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals impending economic contraction, triggering corporate earnings revisions downward and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates weakening aggregate demand momentum, which reduces future revenue growth expectations for cyclical equities and depresses forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance after controlling for single-series effects, or the joint signal shows no directional predictability over 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates supply-side resilience that outpaces demand, leading to margin compression for industrial firms and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when INDPRO rises while PAYEMS stabilizes, or the single-series IC for INDPRO exceeds 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above the central bank's implicit target forces market pricing of prolonged restrictive policy, elevating the equity risk premium and depressing valuation multiples, which yields negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive forward returns in over 55% of observations, or the cross-sectional Rank IC for CPIAUCSL remains below 0.02 across all rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, directly supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly forecasting window.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods of positive GDP growth, or if the rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 for three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate reflects a tightening labor market that accelerates wage inflation, prompting market expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines in out-of-sample prediction, or forward returns are positive when PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating pace of federal funds rate increases compresses equity duration values and raises borrowing costs for leveraged firms, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within a weekly window during active tightening phases.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if weekly forward returns are positive during consecutive rate hike periods, or if the predictive power of FEDFUNDS changes vanishes after controlling for CPIAUCSL.
